name: Lysosomal Restoration Coverage Assessment for June 13, 2026

assessment_window.basis: Backfill assessment of the published June 1 lysosomal-restoration baseline plus the June 7 no-op surveillance session; no new external source search was run for this assessment.

summary: Coverage is adequate for the current low-confidence human_biomarker_signal public claim because the local map contains disease-specific human ambroxol CNS exposure and beta-glucocerebrosidase target-engagement evidence, a randomized Parkinson disease dementia target-engagement trial that constrains functional interpretation, ASPro-PD phase 3 activity context, direct mouse and C. elegans aging-biology evidence for lysosomal function and chaperone-mediated autophagy, and trehalose/TFEB pharmacological tractability in lysosomal storage disease models. Coverage is not strong because human evidence is concentrated in Parkinson disease and Parkinson disease dementia rather than broad aging; controlled ambroxol data do not show cognitive or clinical benefit; much of the aging support is genetic, worm, tissue-specific, disease-model, or stress-response evidence; several additional ambroxol or trehalose disease-program registries remain no-results context; long-term chronic safety and tissue-specific risks are not deeply mapped; and formal recent-review, observational, or seminal-anchor triangulation has not been recorded. The June 7 surveillance pass found no material post-publication delta, so ordinary surveillance can continue.

evidence_categories.rationale: The public stage is anchored by ambroxol human studies: an open-label Parkinson disease trial showing safety, CSF exposure, and glucocerebrosidase-related target engagement; a randomized Parkinson disease dementia phase 2 trial showing beta-glucocerebrosidase target engagement without cognitive or clinical benefit; and ASPro-PD phase 3 protocol activity without results.
evidence_categories.gap_note: Human coverage is adequate only for biomarker-stage target engagement; it does not establish broad aging, healthy older-adult, functional, disease-modifying, or mortality benefit.

evidence_categories.rationale: The assessed artifacts do not systematically map observational ambroxol, Gaucher/GBA, Parkinson progression, lysosomal biomarker, lysosomal-storage-disease, real-world safety, or natural-history cohorts.
evidence_categories.gap_note: This does not block the current intervention-driven public stage, but observational disease-program or biomarker follow-up could help bound target engagement, safety, and phenotype selection.

evidence_categories.rationale: The local map includes direct mammalian aging evidence from LAMP-2A preservation in old mouse liver, C. elegans evidence that lysosomal function declines with age and is required for longevity pathways, LySR/vha-6 RNAi worm healthspan evidence, and trehalose/TFEB Batten disease mouse survival support.
evidence_categories.gap_note: Animal coverage is biologically coherent but heterogeneous across worm, tissue-specific mouse aging, storage-disease mouse survival, genetic preservation, RNAi perturbation, and pharmacological TFEB activation.

evidence_categories.rationale: Mechanistic coverage spans LAMP-2A and chaperone-mediated autophagy, lysosomal morphology, motility, acidity, degradation and gene-expression decline, lysosomal surveillance response after partial v-ATPase perturbation, TFEB activation with trehalose, and ambroxol/GCase target engagement in human Parkinson contexts.
evidence_categories.gap_note: Mechanistic coherence does not define one mature intervention class, and GCase chaperoning, TFEB activation, CMA restoration, lysosome repair, and lysosomal stress responses should not be collapsed into one clinical claim.

evidence_categories.rationale: The map explicitly includes limiting evidence: the randomized Parkinson disease dementia ambroxol trial demonstrated target engagement but no group differences on primary or secondary cognitive and clinical outcomes; ASPro-PD has no efficacy results; trehalose/TFEB evidence is disease-model rather than normal aging; LAMP-2A evidence is tissue-specific and genetic; and LySR evidence is worm RNAi stress-response biology.
evidence_categories.gap_note: A broader failed-program, no-results registry, and adverse-event map across ambroxol, trehalose, TFEB, and other lysosomal interventions is not represented as public evidence.

evidence_categories.rationale: The baseline captures ambroxol short- to medium-term tolerability in Parkinson disease contexts and flags broader chronic lysosomal-activation risk. It does not deeply map long-term preventive safety, neurologic, immune, cancer, metabolic, substrate-specific, tissue-specific, or overactivation risks for chronic lysosomal, TFEB, CMA, v-ATPase, or GCase-directed interventions.
evidence_categories.gap_note: Do not strengthen confidence or assign LEV relevance without longer-duration safety, durability, dose-response, tissue-specific target engagement, and functional outcome data.

evidence_categories.rationale: The June 7 surveillance pass rechecked the three registry-linked ambroxol studies. ASPro-PD NCT05778617 remained recruiting with no posted results and estimated 2029 completion; NCT02941822 remained completed without a registry results section while the peer-reviewed open-label result is public; and NCT02914366 remained unknown status without a registry results section while the peer-reviewed PDD result is public. Broad registry screens also surfaced additional no-results ambroxol and trehalose disease-program context.
evidence_categories.gap_note: Continue watching ASPro-PD/NCT05778617, AMBITIOUS/GBA-PD NCT05287503, Lewy body dementia ambroxol NCT04588285, ambroxol disease-program registries, and SLS-005/trehalose registries for posted results.

evidence_categories.rationale: The backfill reviewed local bootstrap and surveillance artifacts. The June 7 surveillance pass found lysosomal aging, mitochondria-lysosome contact, and lysosomal-control review context, but no formal review, guideline, meta-analysis, or comparative triangulation has been reconciled against the seven-source public map.
evidence_categories.gap_note: A future source-completeness pass should compare recent lysosomal aging, ambroxol/GBA, TFEB, trehalose, CMA, lysosomal repair, LySR, and mitochondria-lysosome review literature against the current source map.

evidence_categories.rationale: The baseline includes plausible anchors for LAMP-2A/CMA restoration in old mouse liver, age-related lysosomal decline and longevity pathways in C. elegans, the LySR/vha-6 RNAi healthspan source, trehalose/TFEB disease-model tractability, open-label ambroxol Parkinson target engagement, randomized ambroxol PDD target engagement without clinical benefit, and ASPro-PD phase 3 activity.
evidence_categories.gap_note: Formal must-include, review-cited, guideline-cited, registry-network, or citation-network auditing could still change source-completeness confidence.

evidence_categories.rationale: The outlook, bootstrap session, surveillance log, and reviews distinguish lysosomal restoration from autophagy induction, selective autophagy, chaperone and heat-shock support, proteostasis, mitochondrial-lysosome contacts, senolytics, disease-specific Parkinson/GBA therapy, storage-disease programs, and generic stress-response or lysosome-repair mechanisms.
evidence_categories.gap_note: Boundary maintenance remains important because future records may be primarily autophagy induction, selective autophagy, proteostasis, lysosome repair, Parkinson disease, storage disease, or mitochondrial quality-control evidence.

search_log_summary: This backfill reviewed local project artifacts: the published Lysosomal Restoration outlook, June 1 bootstrap session, published candidate bundle, source-fidelity and interpretation reviews, publication event, and June 7 no-op surveillance session with its local trial audit, ClinicalTrials.gov, PubMed, correction/retraction, bioRxiv/medRxiv, web, sponsor, registry, and exclusion logs. No new PubMed, registry, preprint, citation-network, or web source search was run for this assessment.
source_selection_notes: PMID 31930374 and PMID 40587145 are the human biomarker-stage anchors for ambroxol CNS exposure and beta-glucocerebrosidase target engagement in Parkinson disease contexts.
PMID 40587145 also provides the key functional boundary because randomized PDD target engagement did not translate into cognitive or clinical outcome differences.
PMID 41708985 is phase 3 protocol and activity evidence only; it supports momentum but not efficacy.
PMID 18690243, PMID 32482227, and PMID 40571723 anchor aging-relevant lysosomal biology in old mouse liver and C. elegans.
PMID 28165011 anchors pharmacological lysosomal-clearance tractability through trehalose/TFEB, but in storage-disease models rather than normal aging.

The June 7 surveillance pass found no material post-publication source, no correction or retraction for baseline PMIDs, no posted ASPro-PD or related ambroxol result, and no same-window lysosomal-restoration evidence that changed the public baseline.

known_gaps.description: Human evidence demonstrates ambroxol CNS exposure and GCase-related target engagement in Parkinson contexts, but controlled data do not show cognitive or clinical benefit and no broad aging-functional trial is staged.
known_gaps.suggested_action: Keep the public stage at human_biomarker_signal until randomized human trials connect lysosomal target engagement to durable function, disease modification, resilience, or aging-relevant outcomes.

known_gaps.description: The human layer is concentrated in Parkinson disease, Parkinson disease dementia, GBA/GCase biology, and lysosomal-disorder program context rather than healthy aging or broad lysosomal decline.
known_gaps.suggested_action: Treat ambroxol evidence as disease-specific target engagement and require aging-relevant or phenotype-enriched human evidence before broadening claims.

known_gaps.description: Long-term safety, dose-response, tissue specificity, substrate specificity, and overactivation risks are unresolved for chronic lysosomal, TFEB, CMA, v-ATPase, LySR, and GCase-directed strategies.
known_gaps.suggested_action: Do not strengthen confidence or assign LEV-relevant timing without long-duration safety, durability, tissue-specific biomarker, and clinical outcome evidence.

known_gaps.description: Animal and model evidence spans old mouse liver, worms, storage-disease mice, RNAi perturbation, TFEB activation, and lysosomal stress responses, making translation and class-level claims uncertain.
known_gaps.suggested_action: Keep animal findings as mechanistic and plausibility support unless mammalian aging interventions show replicated functional benefit with deployable dosing.

known_gaps.description: ASPro-PD remains recruiting with no posted results, and additional ambroxol or trehalose registries such as NCT05287503, NCT04588285, NCT04388969, NCT06614894, NCT07285369, NCT05332678, and NCT05490563 are no-results or disease-program context.
known_gaps.suggested_action: Continue ordinary surveillance for ASPro-PD, AMBITIOUS/GBA-PD, Lewy body dementia ambroxol, disease-program ambroxol, and SLS-005/trehalose posted results or material status changes.

known_gaps.description: Observational disease-program follow-up, real-world ambroxol exposure, GBA/GCase stratification, lysosomal biomarker trajectories, and safety cohorts have not been systematically mapped.
known_gaps.suggested_action: Use a future source-completeness pass to decide whether observational or registry follow-up evidence adds useful safety, target-engagement, or phenotype-selection context.

known_gaps.description: Recent review, guideline, meta-analysis, and citation-network triangulation across lysosomal aging, ambroxol/GBA, TFEB, trehalose, CMA, LySR, lysosome repair, and mitochondria-lysosome contacts is not recorded.
known_gaps.suggested_action: Compare recent review and citation-network coverage against the seven-source public map in a later source-completeness pass.

known_gaps.description: Lysosomal restoration overlaps with autophagy induction, selective autophagy, chaperone-mediated proteostasis, mitochondrial quality control, Parkinson disease programs, storage-disease interventions, and generic stress-response biology.
known_gaps.suggested_action: Keep assigning records by mechanism, target, disease context, endpoint, and aging relevance, and avoid class-level upgrades from adjacent lysosome biology alone.

next_coverage_action: Continue ordinary surveillance for ASPro-PD and other ambroxol or trehalose disease-program results, human lysosomal target engagement tied to function, safety and durability signals, mammalian aging interventions, lysosome repair or LySR translation, TFEB/CMA evidence, and taxonomy-boundary records. Reserve coverage repair for a later source-completeness pass focused on no-results registry representation, observational follow-up, recent-review triangulation, formal seminal-anchor auditing, and broader safety/null maps if no material delta appears.
